note: ISO 15924 script code -> writing-system family for the script-family facet (spec 043 US3, FR-032), which also guards combining-mark-repertoire (T061). Families: alphabet (segmental, independent vowels), abugida (consonant+inherent-vowel, matra-modified), abjad (consonant-only core), syllabary (glyph = whole syllable), logographic (glyph = morpheme/word). A code absent here yields an undetermined script-family (never a guessed family). Deterministic in-repo table, no network (FR-004).

deliberatelyUndetermined.Plrd: Pollard/Miao — contested alphabet/abugida/semisyllabary; no consensus classification (SC-004).
deliberatelyUndetermined.Cpmn: Cypro-Minoan — undeciphered script; no classification possible.
deliberatelyUndetermined.Brai: Braille — tactile transliteration code, not a writing system with an inherent family.
deliberatelyUndetermined.Xpeo: Old Persian — semi-syllabic cuneiform; does not cleanly fit the five families.
deliberatelyUndetermined.Merc: Meroitic Cursive — documented alphabetic-syllabic hybrid; no single family fits.
deliberatelyUndetermined.Mero: Meroitic Hieroglyphic — documented alphabetic-syllabic hybrid; no single family fits.
deliberatelyUndetermined.Hmng: Pahawh Hmong — idiosyncratic onset-rime structure; does not fit the five families.
